name: Acme Pawn Original
description_short: Family-owned Colorado Springs pawn shop since 1976 offering collateral-based cash loans and buying/selling used merchandise across jewelry, firearms, electronics, and collectibles.
description_long:
Acme Pawn Original is a family-owned pawn shop operating in Colorado Springs since 1976, now in its 44th year of business with five locations. The company is managed by second and third-generation family members and has built its reputation as a community-trusted alternative lending and merchandise retailer. The business model combines short-term collateral-based cash loans with retail sales of used and new merchandise.

Acme Pawn offers a broad range of pawn loan services and retail merchandise across multiple categories. Customers can pawn or sell jewelry (gold, silver, diamonds, watches, platinum), firearms and ammunition (handguns, pistols, shotguns, tactical and sport rifles), musical instruments (guitars, drums, saxophones, brass), power tools, bicycles, sports equipment, coins, antiques, collectibles, computers, laptops, tablets, gaming systems, TVs, cameras, and kitchen appliances. The company claims to pay competitive rates, advertising "top dollar" pawn loans and "10% above cost" on brand new firearms. They also purchase broken jewelry and damaged precious metals.

Acme Pawn distinguishes itself through family ownership, longevity, and breadth of merchandise categories. Their website emphasizes friendly and knowledgeable staff, active family involvement in daily operations, a large selection of name-brand used goods, and a blog documenting new inventory and deals. The company positions itself as "the best pawn shop in Colorado Springs, and probably all of Colorado" and appeals to both borrowers seeking quick cash and bargain shoppers. The website features social media presence (Facebook, Instagram) and a mailing list subscription for deal announcements.

However, the company remains a pawn shop, which by definition involves collateral-based lending at rates and terms that may not be disclosed on their website. While five-location presence and 44+ years suggest stability, typical pawn loan terms include high interest rates, short repayment windows, and risk of losing collateral. The website does not disclose interest rates, fees, or loan terms, which is standard for the pawn industry but represents a significant caveat for borrowers. No information about licensing, regulatory compliance, or dispute resolution processes is provided.
address: 2311 E Platte Ave, Colorado Springs, CO 80909

company_info.headquarters: 2311 E Platte Ave, Colorado Springs, CO 80909

pros: Family-owned and operated since 1976 with 44+ years of community presence in Colorado Springs
Five locations across Colorado Springs providing multiple access points
Broad merchandise categories (jewelry, firearms, instruments, tools, electronics, collectibles) appeal to diverse sellers and shoppers
Claims to pay "top dollar" on pawn loans and "10% above cost" on brand new firearms
Active second and third-generation family management involved in daily operations
Purchases broken jewelry and damaged precious metals, expanding what can be pawned
Blog and social media presence provide transparency on inventory and current deals
cons: No interest rates, fees, or loan terms disclosed on website—standard for pawn shops but opaque for borrowers
Risk of losing collateral if loan repayment deadline is missed, a core pawn shop feature
No information about licensing, regulatory compliance, or consumer protection policies
Website emphasizes retail appeal ("save big," "great deals") but does not clearly separate lending vs. retail services
Recent mention of "Top Dollar Pawn Raid" by FBI, U.S. Marshalls, and local police suggests regulatory scrutiny in the local market
best_for: Individuals needing quick cash loans against collateral they own (jewelry, firearms, instruments, electronics)
Budget-conscious shoppers looking for discounted used merchandise across multiple categories
Jewelry sellers or those with broken gold/silver seeking fair market value without traditional resale channels
Firearms buyers seeking new or used guns and ammunition at competitive prices
diagnosis: Acme Pawn Original is best for borrowers who own collateral (jewelry, firearms, electronics, instruments) and need quick cash, or for bargain shoppers seeking discounted used merchandise. The primary caveat is that pawn loans are short-term, collateral-based, carry undisclosed interest rates and fees, and result in loss of items if repayment is missed—making them expensive compared to traditional credit and suitable only for genuine short-term cash needs.
services: Pawn loans on jewelry (gold, silver, diamonds, watches, platinum)
Pawn loans on firearms and ammunition
Pawn loans on power tools and sports equipment
Pawn loans on electronics (computers, laptops, tablets, gaming systems, TVs, cameras)
Purchase of jewelry at fair market value
Purchase of broken jewelry and damaged precious metals
Retail sale of used and new firearms with stated "10% above cost" pricing on new guns
Retail sale of used jewelry, watches, and precious metal items
Retail sale of used musical instruments
Retail sale of used electronics and appliances
Retail sale of coins, antiques, and collectibles
